title: Exercised
author: Daniel E. Lieberman
subline: Why we run, jump, sit, and sleep the way we do.
customHTML:
<svg viewBox="0 0 1600 2400" preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid slice" style="position:absolute;inset:0;width:100%;height:100%">
  <!-- An evolutionary timeline drawn vertically on the right side. 6 silhouettes: fish, reptile, early mammal, ape, early human, modern human. The modern human silhouette is filled WHITE; the others are hairline. One tiny red dot on the modern human's chest — the heart that's being exercised. -->
  <g transform="translate(1280 700)">
    <!-- Connecting vertical 'evolution' line -->
    <line x1="0" y1="0" x2="0" y2="900" stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="1"/>
    <!-- Era labels left side -->
    <g font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif" font-size="11" font-weight="400" fill="#4c4c4c" letter-spacing="0.04em">
      <text x="-160" y="50" text-anchor="end">500 MYA</text>
      <text x="-160" y="200" text-anchor="end">300 MYA</text>
      <text x="-160" y="350" text-anchor="end">65 MYA</text>
      <text x="-160" y="500" text-anchor="end">7 MYA</text>
      <text x="-160" y="650" text-anchor="end">3 MYA</text>
      <text x="-160" y="800" text-anchor="end">NOW</text>
    </g>
    <!-- Fish (500 MYA) -->
    <g transform="translate(0 50)" fill="none" stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="1">
      <path d="M -40 0 Q -20 -10 0 0 Q 20 10 40 0 L 30 0 Q 25 5 20 0"/>
      <line x1="20" y1="-3" x2="40" y2="-10"/>
    </g>
    <!-- Reptile (300 MYA) -->
    <g transform="translate(0 200)" fill="none" stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="1">
      <ellipse cx="0" cy="0" rx="40" ry="6"/>
      <line x1="-25" y1="6" x2="-25" y2="14"/>
      <line x1="-15" y1="6" x2="-15" y2="14"/>
      <line x1="15" y1="6" x2="15" y2="14"/>
      <line x1="25" y1="6" x2="25" y2="14"/>
      <line x1="-40" y1="0" x2="-50" y2="-2"/>
    </g>
    <!-- Early mammal (65 MYA) -->
    <g transform="translate(0 350)" fill="none" stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="1">
      <ellipse cx="0" cy="0" rx="25" ry="12"/>
      <line x1="20" y1="-3" x2="40" y2="-10"/>
      <line x1="-15" y1="8" x2="-15" y2="14"/>
      <line x1="15" y1="8" x2="15" y2="14"/>
    </g>
    <!-- Ape (7 MYA) -->
    <g transform="translate(0 500)" fill="none" stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="1">
      <ellipse cx="0" cy="-5" rx="14" ry="18"/>
      <line x1="-5" y1="13" x2="-12" y2="20"/>
      <line x1="5" y1="13" x2="12" y2="20"/>
      <line x1="0" y1="-22" x2="-3" y2="-30"/>
      <line x1="-3" y1="-22" x2="-10" y2="-28"/>
    </g>
    <!-- Early human (3 MYA) -->
    <g transform="translate(0 650)" fill="none" stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="1">
      <circle cx="0" cy="-22" r="8"/>
      <line x1="0" y1="-14" x2="0" y2="10"/>
      <line x1="-7" y1="-5" x2="-15" y2="5"/>
      <line x1="7" y1="-5" x2="15" y2="5"/>
      <line x1="0" y1="10" x2="-8" y2="22"/>
      <line x1="0" y1="10" x2="8" y2="22"/>
    </g>
    <!-- Modern human (NOW) — white filled silhouette, running -->
    <g transform="translate(0 800)" fill="#ffffff">
      <circle cx="0" cy="-30" r="10"/>
      <path d="M 0 -20 L -8 0 L -18 18 M 0 -20 L 8 0 L 18 18 M 0 -20 L 0 5 M 0 5 L -12 18 M 0 5 L 12 18"/>
    </g>
    <!-- The red heart dot on the modern human -->
    <circle cx="0" cy="775" r="5" fill="#fc1c46"/>
    <text x="20" y="780" font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif" font-size="10" font-weight="500" fill="#fc1c46" letter-spacing="0.18em">PULSE</text>
  </g>
  <!-- Top label -->
  <text x="1280" y="380" text-anchor="middle" font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif" font-size="14" font-weight="500" fill="#4c4c4c" letter-spacing="0.18em">THE LOCOMOTOR TREE</text>
</svg>
rationale: An evolutionary timeline of locomotion: fish, reptile, mammal, ape, early human, and a running modern human. The earlier five are hairline graphite; the modern human is white-filled, running, with a small red dot on the chest — the pulse. The only red. The title 'EXERCISED' is set at a massive 460px uppercase, weight 500, sitting at the top of the canvas. The book's thesis: the human body is the product of evolutionary pressures — exercise is built into our design.
